title: SCHC: Missing CoAP version validation during compression
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/schc/schc.c
Lines: 736-806 (compress_coap), 303-310 (coap_write_fixed)

During CoAP compression, the code extracts type and tkl from the CoAP header but does not validate that the CoAP version is 1. During decompression, coap_write_fixed() hardcodes version 1:

```c
coap[SCHC_COAP_VER_TYPE_TKL_OFFSET] =
    (1u << 6) | ((type & 0x3u) << 4) | (tkl & 0x0Fu);  // Version hardcoded to 1
```

Impact:
- If a packet with CoAP version != 1 is compressed and decompressed, the version changes to 1
- This is lossy compression that could corrupt packets
- RFC 7252 only defines version 1, so currently valid packets are unaffected
- Risk is future CoAP versions or non-compliant implementations

Fix: Either:
1. Validate CoAP version == 1 during compression (return SCHC_ERR_NO_MATCHING_RULE for others)
2. Store and restore the version in the residue

Severity is low because RFC 7252 only defines version 1.
